non_blocking_risks.risk: If governance-checkpoints.json changes after xml-rewrite.xml is generated, the export candidate may need to be regenerated or re-reviewed.

profile_adherence.evidence: The run used co-create steady pacing, which matched the approval-heavy export round and kept interaction focused on meaningful release checkpoints.

same_source_consistency.evidence: The approval checkpoint, override policy, and exported representation all refer to the same bounded output object.

cross_format_consistency.evidence: The XML export and representation bundle refer to the same release candidate already bounded by governance artifacts.

fact_validation_chain.item: Confirm xml-rewrite.xml exists as a concrete export candidate.
Confirm governance-checkpoints.json declares review points and non-overridable constraints.
Confirm override-policy.json defines how human review may block, approve, or regenerate exports.
Confirm representation-bundle.json is not treated as final before the approval boundary is satisfied.

unverifiable_assumptions.item: The human reviewer will actually inspect the same exported payload that the current xml-rewrite.xml records.

check.note: governance-checkpoints.json and override-policy.json exist and define approval boundaries.
xml-rewrite.xml and representation-bundle.json exist as the current export payload and bundle index.

scope_alignment_checks.item: The round stays on human review and export authorization logic and does not widen into runtime governance operations.

check.note: xml-rewrite.xml can be prepared early, but remains a candidate until governance approval is complete.
governance-checkpoints.json and override-policy.json exist before the export is treated as final.

dispatch_recommendations.item: Proceed because export construction and export authorization are explicitly separated and governed.

check.why: Without it, the export approval boundary and non-overridable constraints would remain implicit.
check.evidence: mode-resolution.json places governance above export authorization timing.

check.why: It is the concrete payload under review, bridging construction and approval.
check.evidence: Both governance-checkpoints.json and representation-bundle.json refer to it conceptually.

profile_adjustment_recommendation.adjustment_reason: The current profile already matched approval-oriented export work and did not show a need for either deeper probing or more frequent user interruption.

evaluation_report.score_reason: The example cleanly covers approval-heavy export governance, but still assumes a relatively simple review topology rather than a multi-reviewer approval chain.
